meta.notes: Self-hosted AI agent gateway. Connects messaging channels (WhatsApp, Telegram, Discord, iMessage, Signal) to AI agents. Requires Node 22+.
meta.tldr: This release introduces forward-compatibility for `gpt-5.4-pro` models and surfaces human topic names in Telegram forum contexts. A significant security enhancement restricts the model-facing gateway tool from enabling potentially dangerous flags via `config.patch` or `config.apply`, requiring alternative methods for enabling such flags. Additionally, the release normalizes browser SSRF policies to preserve strict mode for existing configurations and canonicalizes Feishu allowlist entries to prevent unintended matching behavior. Several fixes address issues with Ollama timeouts, Codex model validation, image and PDF tool model references, Slack interaction allowlists, media attachment path resolution, Google image generation, Telegram topic persistence, systemd dotenv secrets, Ollama usage reporting, plugin catalog lookups, GitHub Copilot reasoning, memory embedding providers, local model hints, browser SSRF policy, onboarding verification probes, subagent registry imports, media-understanding proxy environments, Telegram media downloads, browser CDP readiness, context engine compaction, Codex CLI auth diagnostics, Discord native commands, Ollama session memory slug generation, media transcription, WhatsApp media uploads, UI markdown parsing, auto-reply send policy, BlueBubbles server-info cache, heartbeat security, Microsoft Teams SSO security, config redaction, context engine maintenance, plugin status reporting, context engine validation, WhatsApp media encryption, gateway update paths, heartbeat Telegram topics, browser CDP policy, gateway session metadata, cron scheduler retries, outbound delivery queue context, memory Ollama adapter, auto-reply queue authorization, Dreaming cron events, Control UI Dreaming, agent tool retries, active memory display, memory QMD recall, agent subagent runtime, agent OpenAI reasoning, voice call media stream, Telegram status commands, TTS reply media, agent tool paths, agent OpenAI embedded runs, outbound relay status, Slack doctor contract, session memory hooks, CLI approvals timeout, media store limits, plugin setup entry, CLI update pruning, and gateway exec event deduplication. Users relying on the model-facing gateway tool to enable restricted flags should review their configurations. It is recommended to upgrade to this release to benefit from the security enhancements and bug fixes. Verify Feishu allowlist configurations and browser SSRF policies after upgrading.

features.name: Gateway Service
features.description: The core Gateway process bridges messaging channels to AI agents. Runs as a background daemon service. Single process serves all channels simultaneously.
key_details: openclaw gateway --force — force-kill listener then start
openclaw gateway status — check running status
Managed as system daemon with openclaw gateway start/stop/restart

features.name: Multi-Channel Support
features.description: One Gateway serves multiple messaging channels simultaneously. Each channel connects independently.

features.name: Multi-Agent Routing
features.description: Run multiple named agents. Each agent gets its own session context, model, system prompt, and channel routing. Switch between agents or dispatch tasks.
key_details: Named agents: builder, claw, main, isolated, etc.
Each agent: own model, system prompt, memory, session state
Agent definitions: AGENTS.md + SOUL.md in agent workspace dir
ACP harness sessions via sessions_spawn with runtime:acp

features.name: Agent Skills (AgentSkills Standard)
features.description: Pluggable capabilities using the AgentSkills open standard. Skills are SKILL.md files that inject specialized context when activated.
key_details: Install skills from clawhub.com via the clawhub CLI
SKILL.md format: YAML frontmatter + markdown body
Skills appear in agent system prompt discovery section
openclaw skills list — list installed skills

features.name: Memory System
features.description: Persistent agent memory via MEMORY.md and memory/*.md files. Agents recall prior decisions, preferences, and context across sessions.
key_details: Primary: MEMORY.md in agent workspace root
memory_search tool: semantic search across MEMORY.md + memory/*.md
memory_get tool: targeted line reads from memory files
Agents required to run memory_search before answering questions about prior work

features.name: Session Management
features.description: Full session lifecycle management. Sessions persist across gateway restarts. Multi-session routing per channel.
key_details: openclaw sessions list — list active sessions
sessions_list — list all sessions with filters

features.name: Cron Scheduler
features.description: Gateway-level cron job scheduling for reminders, recurring agent tasks, and timed events.
key_details: cron tool: add, remove, list, run, update jobs
Payload types: systemEvent (main session), agentTurn (isolated session)
sessionTarget: main (systemEvent only) or isolated (agentTurn only)
Webhook delivery: POST finished-run event to delivery.to URL

features.name: Paired Nodes
features.description: Pair external devices (phones, laptops, Raspberry Pi) as nodes. Camera, screen recording, location, notifications, and remote command execution.
key_details: nodes tool: status, describe, camera_snap, screen_record, location_get, notifications_list, run
Pairing via QR code or link
Remote command execution on paired node

features.name: Browser Control
features.description: Full browser automation via OpenClaw Browser Control Server. Profiles: openclaw (isolated) and chrome (relay via extension).
key_details: browser tool: status, start, stop, tabs, open, snapshot, screenshot, act
Profile chrome: Chrome extension relay (existing tabs)
act tool for UI automation: click, type, press, hover, drag, select
snapshot: accessibility tree (aria or role refs)

features.name: Plugin System
features.description: Community plugins that extend Gateway capabilities with new channels, tools, and integrations.
key_details: openclaw plugins list — list installed plugins
Plugins: agent-tools, voice-call, mattermost, zalouser, others

features.name: ACP (Agent Communication Protocol)
features.description: ACP harness integration for spawning and managing external coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, OpenCode) from within OpenClaw sessions.
key_details: Allowed agents listed via agents_list tool
ACP sessions run in isolated workspace

features.name: Control UI / Dashboard
features.description: Browser-based chat and management dashboard. Opens at http://127.0.0.1:18789/ by default.
key_details: openclaw dashboard — open Control UI in browser
Chat with agents directly in browser (no channel setup needed)

features.name: Secrets Management
features.description: SecretRef contract for runtime secret injection. Secrets applied via openclaw secrets apply, referenced as ${{secret.name}} in config.
key_details: openclaw secrets apply — apply secrets to runtime
Runtime snapshot behavior: secrets not written to disk
openclaw secrets list — list known secret keys

features.name: Sandbox
features.description: Command execution sandbox for agent tool calls. Controls which shell commands agents can run without approval.

features.name: Chrome DevTools MCP attach mode
features.description: Add an official Chrome DevTools MCP attach mode for signed-in live Chrome sessions, with docs for `chrome://inspect/#remote-debugging` enablement and direct backlinks to Chrome’s own setup guides.

features.name: Built-in browser profiles
features.description: Add built-in `profile="user"` for the logged-in host browser and `profile="chrome-relay"` for the extension relay, so agent browser calls can prefer the real signed-in browser without the extra `browserSession` selector.

features.name: Batched browser act automation
features.description: Add batched actions, selector targeting, and delayed clicks for browser act requests with normalized batch dispatch.

features.name: Android Chat Settings UI Redesign
features.description: Redesigned chat settings UI for the Android app.

features.name: iOS Onboarding Welcome Pager
features.description: Added an onboarding welcome pager to the iOS app.

features.name: Slack Interactive Reply Directives
features.description: Added opt-in interactive reply directives for Slack.

features.name: Docker Timezone Support
features.description: Added timezone support to Docker using the OPENCLAW_TZ environment variable.

features.name: Image Generation Provider
features.description: Adds an image generation provider for the `image-01` model, supporting generate and image-to-image editing with aspect ratio control.

features.name: Async requireApproval Hooks
features.description: Adds async `requireApproval` to `before_tool_call` hooks, letting plugins pause tool execution and prompt the user for approval via various channels.

features.name: Slack upload-file action
features.description: Adds an explicit `upload-file` Slack action that routes file uploads through the existing Slack upload transport, with optional filename/title/comment overrides for channels and DMs.

features.name: File-first sends
features.description: Unifies file-first sends on the canonical `upload-file` action by adding explicit support for Microsoft Teams and Google Chat, and by exposing BlueBubbles file sends through `upload-file` while keeping the legacy `sendAttachment` alias.

features.name: Matrix TTS voice bubbles
features.description: Sends auto-TTS replies as native Matrix voice bubbles instead of generic audio attachments.

features.name: openclaw config schema
features.description: Adds `openclaw config schema` to print the generated JSON schema for `openclaw.json`.

features.name: Tavily client source header
features.description: Marks outbound API requests with `X-Client-Source: openclaw` so Tavily can attribute OpenClaw-originated traffic.

features.name: Gateway-backed channel MCP bridge
features.description: Adds a Gateway-backed channel MCP bridge with Codex/Claude-facing conversation tools, Claude channel notifications, and safer stdio bridge lifecycle handling for reconnects and routed session discovery.

features.name: x_search
features.description: Adds first-class `x_search` to the bundled xAI provider via the Responses API, and auto-enables the xAI plugin from owned web-search and tool config.

features.name: Current-Conversation ACP Binds
features.description: Adds current-conversation ACP binds for Discord, BlueBubbles, and iMessage so `/acp spawn codex --bind here` can turn the current chat into a Codex-backed workspace without creating a child thread.

features.name: Gemini CLI Backend Support
features.description: Adds bundled Gemini CLI backend support and moves bundled Claude CLI, Codex CLI, and Gemini CLI inference defaults onto the plugin surface.

features.name: Microsoft Teams and Google Chat File Support
features.description: Adds explicit support for Microsoft Teams and Google Chat file-first sends on the canonical `upload-file` action.

features.name: Plugin Runtime runHeartbeatOnce
features.description: Exposes `runHeartbeatOnce` in the plugin runtime `system` namespace so plugins can trigger a single heartbeat cycle with an explicit delivery target override.

features.name: pnpm docs:check-links:anchors
features.description: Adds `pnpm docs:check-links:anchors` for Mintlify anchor validation while keeping `scripts/docs-link-audit.mjs` as the stable link-audit entrypoint.

features.name: Async requireApproval hooks
features.description: Plugins can now pause tool execution and prompt the user for approval via the exec approval overlay, Telegram buttons, Discord interactions, or the `/approve` command.

features.name: Matrix Streaming
features.description: Add `streaming: "partial"` draft replies that stay on a single editable preview message, stop preview streaming once text no longer fits one Matrix event, and clear stale previews before media-only finals.

features.name: CLI Config Schema
features.description: CLI command `openclaw config schema` to print the generated JSON schema for `openclaw.json`.

features.name: Responses API
features.description: Move the bundled xAI provider to the Responses API.

features.name: Async requireApproval in before_tool_call hooks
features.description: Add async `requireApproval` to `before_tool_call` hooks, letting plugins pause tool execution and prompt the user for approval.

features.name: Explicit upload-file Slack action
features.description: Add an explicit `upload-file` Slack action that routes file uploads through the existing Slack upload transport, with optional filename/title/comment overrides for channels and DMs.

features.name: Durable lifecycle records for ACP/subagent spawned work
features.description: Keep durable lifecycle records for ACP/subagent spawned work and deliver ACP completion/failure updates through the real requester chat path instead of session-only stream events.

features.name: Image Generation
features.description: Adds image generation provider for `image-01` model, supporting generate and image-to-image editing with aspect ratio control.

features.name: Current Conversation ACP Binds for Discord, BlueBubbles, and iMessage
features.description: Adds current-conversation ACP binds for Discord, BlueBubbles, and iMessage so `/acp spawn codex --bind here` can turn the current chat into a Codex-backed workspace without creating a child thread.

features.name: Image Generation Provider for MiniMax
features.description: Adds image generation provider for `image-01` model, supporting generate and image-to-image editing with aspect ratio control.

features.name: Async requireApproval in before_tool_call hooks
features.description: Adds async `requireApproval` to `before_tool_call` hooks, letting plugins pause tool execution and prompt the user for approval.

features.name: File-first sends for Microsoft Teams and Google Chat
features.description: Adds explicit support for Microsoft Teams and Google Chat to the canonical `upload-file` action.

features.name: Matrix streaming partial draft replies
features.description: Adds `streaming: "partial"` draft replies that stay on a single editable preview message, stop preview streaming once text no longer fits one Matrix event, and clear stale previews before media-only finals.

features.name: CLI Backend Logs
features.description: Generic --cli-backend-logs flag to replace gateway run --claude-cli-logs for viewing CLI backend logs.

features.name: Image Generation Provider for image-01
features.description: MiniMax image generation provider for the `image-01` model, supporting generate and image-to-image editing with aspect ratio control.

features.name: QQ Bot Channel Plugin
features.description: Adds QQ Bot as a bundled channel plugin with multi-account setup, SecretRef-aware credentials, slash commands, reminders, and media send/receive support.

features.name: Linear Task Flow Control
features.description: Adds a linear task flow control surface with `openclaw flows list|show|cancel`, keeps manual multi-task flows separate from one-task auto-sync flows, and surfaces doctor recovery hints for obviously orphaned or broken flow/task linkage.

features.name: Microsoft Teams Member Info
features.description: Adds a Graph-backed member info action so Teams automations and tools can resolve channel member details directly from Microsoft Graph.

features.name: Slack Exec Approvals
features.description: Adds native Slack approval routing and approver authorization so exec approval prompts can stay in Slack instead of falling back to the Web UI or terminal.

features.name: WhatsApp Reactions
features.description: Agents can now react with emoji on incoming WhatsApp messages, enabling more natural conversational interactions like acknowledging a photo with ❤️ instead of typing a reply.

features.name: Android Notifications
features.description: Add notification-forwarding controls with package filtering, quiet hours, rate limiting, and safer picker behavior for forwarded notification events.

features.name: Channels/QQ Bot
features.description: Add QQ Bot as a bundled channel plugin with multi-account setup, SecretRef-aware credentials, slash commands, reminders, and media send/receive support.

features.name: LINE/outbound media
features.description: Add LINE image, video, and audio outbound sends on the LINE-specific delivery path, including explicit preview/tracking handling for videos while keeping generic media sends on the existing image-only route.

features.name: Matrix/history
features.description: Add optional room history context for Matrix group triggers via `channels.matrix.historyLimit`, with per-agent watermarks and retry-safe snapshots so failed trigger retries do not drift into newer room messages.

features.name: Matrix/network
features.description: Add explicit `channels.matrix.proxy` config for routing Matrix traffic through an HTTP(S) proxy, including account-level overrides and matching probe/runtime behavior.

features.name: Memory/QMD
features.description: Add per-agent `memorySearch.qmd.extraCollections` so agents can opt into cross-agent session search without flattening every transcript collection into one shared QMD namespace.

features.name: Nostr/inbound DMs
features.description: Verify inbound event signatures before pairing or sender-authorization side effects, so forged DM events no longer create pairing requests or trigger reply attempts.

features.name: Pi/Codex
features.description: Add native Codex web search support for embedded Pi runs, including config/docs/wizard coverage and managed-tool suppression when native Codex search is active.

features.name: TTS
features.description: Add structured provider diagnostics and fallback attempt analytics.

features.name: Android Notification Forwarding Controls
features.description: Add notification-forwarding controls with package filtering, quiet hours, rate limiting, and safer picker behavior for forwarded notification events.

features.name: Background Tasks Shared Control Plane
features.description: Turn tasks into a real shared background-run control plane instead of ACP-only bookkeeping by unifying ACP, subagent, cron, and background CLI execution under one SQLite-backed ledger, routing detached lifecycle updates through the executor seam, adding audit/maintenance/status visibility, tightening auto-cleanup and lost-run recovery, improving task awareness in internal status/tool surfaces, and clarifying the split between heartbeat/main-session automation and detached scheduled runs.

features.name: Matrix Room History Context
features.description: Add optional room history context for Matrix group triggers via `channels.matrix.historyLimit`, with per-agent watermarks and retry-safe snapshots so failed trigger retries do not drift into newer room messages.

features.name: Matrix HTTP Proxy Support
features.description: Add explicit `channels.matrix.proxy` config for routing Matrix traffic through an HTTP(S) proxy, including account-level overrides and matching probe/runtime behavior.

features.name: MCP Remote HTTP/SSE Server Support
features.description: Add remote HTTP/SSE server support for `mcp.servers` URL configs, including auth headers and safer config redaction for MCP credentials.

features.name: Microsoft Teams Graph Member Info
features.description: Add a Graph-backed member info action so Teams automations and tools can resolve channel member details directly from Microsoft Graph.

features.name: Pi Codex Web Search Support
features.description: Add native Codex web search support for embedded Pi runs, including config/docs/wizard coverage and managed-tool suppression when native Codex search is active.

features.name: Slack Native Approval Routing
features.description: Add native Slack approval routing and approver authorization so exec approval prompts can stay in Slack instead of falling back to the Web UI or terminal.

features.name: Chat-native background task board
features.description: Adds `/tasks` as a chat-native background task board for the current session, with recent task details and agent-local fallback counts when no linked tasks are visible.

features.name: SearXNG provider plugin
features.description: Adds the bundled SearXNG provider plugin for `web_search` with configurable host support.

features.name: Bedrock Guardrails support
features.description: Adds Bedrock Guardrails support to the bundled provider.

features.name: Voice Wake option
features.description: Adds the Voice Wake option to trigger Talk Mode.

features.name: Feishu Drive comment-event flow
features.description: Adds a dedicated Drive comment-event flow with comment-thread context resolution, in-thread replies, and `feishu_drive` comment actions for document collaboration workflows.

features.name: Global default provider parameters
features.description: Adds `agents.defaults.params` for global default provider parameters.

features.name: Cron job tool allowlists
features.description: Adds `openclaw cron --tools` for per-job tool allowlists.

features.name: WhatsApp reaction level guidance
features.description: Adds `reactionLevel` guidance for agent reactions.

features.name: ZAI models glm-5.1 and glm-5v-turbo
features.description: Adds `glm-5.1` and `glm-5v-turbo` to the bundled Z.AI provider catalog.

features.name: Web search/SearXNG
features.description: Add the bundled SearXNG provider plugin for `web_search` with configurable host support.

features.name: Amazon Bedrock/Guardrails
features.description: Add Bedrock Guardrails support to the bundled provider.

features.name: macOS/Voice Wake
features.description: Add the Voice Wake option to trigger Talk Mode.

features.name: Feishu/comments
features.description: Add a dedicated Drive comment-event flow with comment-thread context resolution, in-thread replies, and `feishu_drive` comment actions for document collaboration workflows.

features.name: Telegram/errors
features.description: Add configurable `errorPolicy` and `errorCooldownMs` controls so Telegram can suppress repeated delivery errors per account, chat, and topic without muting distinct failures.

features.name: Telegram/errors configurable error policy
features.description: Adds configurable `errorPolicy` and `errorCooldownMs` controls so Telegram can suppress repeated delivery errors per account, chat, and topic without muting distinct failures.

features.name: Android Assistant Integration
features.description: Adds assistant-role entrypoints plus Google Assistant App Actions metadata so Android can launch OpenClaw from the assistant trigger and hand prompts into the chat composer.

features.name: Task Flow
features.description: Restores the core Task Flow substrate with managed-vs-mirrored sync modes, durable flow state/revision tracking, and `openclaw flows` inspection/recovery primitives so background orchestration can persist and be operated separately from plugin authoring layers.

features.name: Managed Child Task Spawning
features.description: Adds managed child task spawning plus sticky cancel intent, so external orchestrators can stop scheduling immediately and let parent Task Flows settle to `cancelled` once active child tasks finish.

features.name: Plugin Task Flow API
features.description: Adds a bound `api.runtime.taskFlow` seam so plugins and trusted authoring layers can create and drive managed Task Flows from host-resolved OpenClaw context without passing owner identifiers on each call.

features.name: Provider Replay Hooks
features.description: Adds provider-owned replay hook surfaces for transcript policy, replay cleanup, and reasoning-mode dispatch.

features.name: Before Agent Reply Hook
features.description: Adds `before_agent_reply` so plugins can short-circuit the LLM with synthetic replies after inline actions.

features.name: Plugin-Owned Session Key Surfaces
features.description: Moves provider-specific session conversation grammar into plugin-owned session-key surfaces, preserving Telegram topic routing and Feishu scoped inheritance across bootstrap, model override, restart, and tool-policy paths.

features.name: Plugin-Owned Viewer Base URL
features.description: Adds plugin-owned `viewerBaseUrl` so viewer links can use a stable proxy/public origin without passing `baseUrl` on every tool call.

features.name: Tasks/Task Flow
features.description: Restores the core Task Flow substrate with managed-vs-mirrored sync modes, durable flow state/revision tracking, and `openclaw tasks flow` inspection/recovery primitives.

features.name: Plugin/Task Flow API
features.description: Adds a bound `api.runtime.taskFlow` seam so plugins and trusted authoring layers can create and drive managed Task Flows from host-resolved OpenClaw context without passing owner identifiers on each call.

features.name: Before Agent Reply Hook
features.description: Adds `before_agent_reply` so plugins can short-circuit the LLM with synthetic replies after inline actions.

features.name: Video Generation Tool
features.description: Adds the built-in `video_generate` tool so agents can create videos through configured providers and return the generated media directly in the reply.

features.name: Music Generation Tool
features.description: Adds the built-in `music_generate` tool with bundled Google (Lyria) and MiniMax providers plus workflow-backed Comfy support, including async task tracking and follow-up delivery of finished audio.

features.name: ComfyUI Workflow Media Plugin
features.description: Adds a bundled `comfy` workflow media plugin for local ComfyUI and Comfy Cloud workflows, including shared `image_generate`, `video_generate`, and workflow-backed `music_generate` support, with prompt injection, optional reference-image upload, live tests, and output download.

features.name: Qwen, Fireworks AI, and StepFun Providers
features.description: Adds bundled Qwen, Fireworks AI, and StepFun providers, plus MiniMax TTS, Ollama Web Search, and MiniMax Search integrations for chat, speech, and search workflows.

features.name: Amazon Bedrock Mantle Support
features.description: Adds bundled Mantle support plus inference-profile discovery and automatic request-region injection so Bedrock-hosted Claude, GPT-OSS, Qwen, Kimi, GLM, and similar routes work with less manual setup.

features.name: Localized Control UI Support
features.description: Adds localized control UI support for Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, Brazilian Portuguese, German, Spanish, Japanese, Korean, French, Turkish, Indonesian, Polish, and Ukrainian.

features.name: ClawHub Skills Panel Integration
features.description: Adds ClawHub search, detail, and install flows directly in the Skills panel.

features.name: iOS Exec Approvals
features.description: Adds generic APNs approval notifications that open an in-app exec approval modal, fetch command details only after authenticated operator reconnect, and clear stale notification state when the approval resolves.

features.name: Matrix Native Exec Approvals
features.description: Adds Matrix-native exec approval prompts with account-scoped approvers, channel-or-DM delivery, and room-thread aware resolution handling.

features.name: Configurable Channel Context Visibility
features.description: Adds configurable `contextVisibility` per channel (`all`, `allowlist`, `allowlist_quote`) so supplemental quote, thread, and fetched history context can be filtered by sender allowlists instead of always passing through as received.

features.name: Shared Model and Media Request Overrides
features.description: Adds shared model and media request transport overrides across OpenAI-, Anthropic-, Google-, and compatible provider paths, including headers, auth, proxy, and TLS controls.

features.name: OpenAI Codex GPT-5.4-mini
features.description: Adds forward-compat `openai-codex/gpt-5.4-mini`, an opt-in GPT personality, and provider-owned GPT-5 prompt contributions so Codex/GPT runs stay cache-stable and compatible with bundled catalog lag.

features.name: ACPX Runtime Embedding
features.description: Embeds the ACP runtime directly in the bundled `acpx` plugin, removes the extra external ACP CLI hop, hardens live ACP session binding and reuse, and adds a generic `reply_dispatch` hook so bundled plugins like ACPX can own reply interception without hardcoded ACP paths in core auto-reply routing.

features.name: Structured Plan and Execution Updates
features.description: Adds experimental structured plan updates and structured execution item events so compatible UIs can show clearer step-by-step progress during long-running runs.

features.name: xAI, Alibaba, and Runway Video Providers
features.description: Adds bundled xAI (`grok-imagine-video`), Alibaba Model Studio Wan, and Runway video providers, plus live-test/default model wiring for all three.

features.name: Amazon Bedrock Embeddings
features.description: Adds Amazon Bedrock embeddings for Titan, Cohere, Nova, and TwelveLabs models, with AWS credential-chain auto-detection for `provider: "auto"` and provider-specific dimension controls.

features.name: Amazon Bedrock Mantle Bearer Tokens
features.description: Generates bearer tokens from the AWS credential chain so Mantle auto-discovery can use IAM auth without manually exporting `AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK`.

features.name: Memory Dreaming
features.description: Adds weighted short-term recall promotion, a `/dreaming` command, Dreams UI, multilingual conceptual tagging, and doctor/status repair support, while refactoring dreaming from competing modes into three cooperative phases (light, deep, REM) with independent schedules and recovery behavior so durable memory promotion can run in the background with less manual setup.

features.name: Comfy workflow media plugin
features.description: Adds a bundled `comfy` workflow media plugin for local ComfyUI and Comfy Cloud workflows, including shared `image_generate`, `video_generate`, and workflow-backed `music_generate` support, with prompt injection, optional reference-image upload, live tests, and output download.

features.name: Arcee AI provider plugin
features.description: Adds a bundled Arcee AI provider plugin with `ARCEEAI_API_KEY` onboarding, Trinity model catalog (mini, large-preview, large-thinking), OpenAI-compatible API support, and OpenRouter as an alternative auth path.

features.name: Memory dreaming (experimental)
features.description: Adds weighted short-term recall promotion, a `/dreaming` command, Dreams UI, multilingual conceptual tagging, and doctor/status repair support, while refactoring dreaming from competing modes into three cooperative phases (light, deep, REM) with independent schedules and recovery behavior so durable memory promotion can run in the background with less manual setup.

features.name: CLI Infer Hub
features.description: A first-class `openclaw infer ...` hub for provider-backed inference workflows across model, media, web, and embedding tasks.

features.name: Memory Wiki Stack
features.description: Restores the bundled `memory-wiki` stack with plugin, CLI, sync/query/apply tooling, memory-host integration, structured claim/evidence fields, compiled digest retrieval, claim-health linting, contradiction clustering, staleness dashboards, and freshness-weighted search.

features.name: Webhook Ingress Plugin
features.description: A bundled webhook ingress plugin so external automation can create and drive bound TaskFlows through per-route shared-secret endpoints.

features.name: Pluggable Compaction Provider Registry
features.description: A pluggable compaction provider registry so plugins can replace the built-in summarization pipeline. Configure via `agents.defaults.compaction.provider`; falls back to LLM summarization on provider failure.

features.name: Agents System Prompt Override
features.description: Adds `agents.defaults.systemPromptOverride` for controlled prompt experiments plus heartbeat prompt-section controls so heartbeat runtime behavior can stay enabled without injecting heartbeat instructions every turn.
key_details: Heartbeat prompt-section controls allow enabling heartbeat runtime behavior without injecting heartbeat instructions every turn.

features.name: Persisted Compaction Checkpoints
features.description: Adds persisted compaction checkpoints plus Sessions UI branch/restore actions so operators can inspect and recover pre-compaction session state.
key_details: Sessions UI branch/restore actions allow inspecting and recovering pre-compaction session state.

features.name: Memory Dreaming Ingest Redacted Session Transcripts
features.description: Ingests redacted session transcripts into the dreaming corpus with per-day session-corpus notes, cursor checkpointing, and promotion/doctor support.
key_details: Redacted session transcripts ingested into the dreaming corpus.
Features per-day session-corpus notes and cursor checkpointing.

features.name: Plugin SDK Provider Auth Runtime
features.description: Exposes runtime-ready provider auth through `openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-auth-runtime` so native plugins and context engines can resolve request-ready credentials after provider-owned runtime exchanges like GitHub Copilot device-token-to-bearer flows.
key_details: Allows native plugins and context engines to resolve request-ready credentials after provider-owned runtime exchanges.

features.name: Character Vibes Evaluation Reports
features.description: Add character-vibes evaluation reports with model selection and parallel runs so live QA can compare candidate behavior faster.

features.name: Provider Auth Aliases
features.description: Let provider manifests declare `providerAuthAliases` so provider variants can share env vars, auth profiles, config-backed auth, and API-key onboarding choices without core-specific wiring.

features.name: Structured Diary View
features.description: Adds a structured diary view with timeline navigation, backfill/reset controls, traceable dreaming summaries, and a grounded Scene lane with promotion hints plus a safe clear-grounded action for staged backfill signals in the Control UI/dreaming.

features.name: Grounded REM Backfill Lane
features.description: Adds a grounded REM backfill lane with historical `rem-harness --path`, diary commit/reset flows, cleaner durable-fact extraction, and live short-term promotion integration so old daily notes can replay into Dreams and durable memory without a second memory stack.

features.name: Active Memory Plugin
features.description: Adds a dedicated memory sub-agent right before the main reply, automatically pulling in relevant preferences, context, and past details without manual user input. Includes configurable message/recent/full context modes, live `/verbose` inspection, advanced prompt/thinking overrides, and opt-in transcript persistence.

features.name: Local MLX Speech Provider
features.description: Adds an experimental local MLX speech provider for Talk Mode, with explicit provider selection, local utterance playback, interruption handling, and system-voice fallback.

features.name: QA Matrix Lane
features.description: Adds a live `openclaw qa matrix` lane backed by a disposable Matrix homeserver, shared live-transport seams, and Matrix-specific transport coverage for threading, reactions, restart, and allowlist behavior.

features.name: QA Telegram Lane
features.description: Adds a live `openclaw qa telegram` lane for private-group bot-to-bot checks, hardens its artifact handling, and preserves native Telegram command reply threading for QA verification.

features.name: Multipass QA Runner
features.description: Adds a `--runner multipass` lane for `openclaw qa suite` so repo-backed QA scenarios can run inside a disposable Linux VM and write back the usual report, summary, and VM logs.

features.name: Commands List RPC
features.description: Adds a `commands.list` RPC so remote gateway clients can discover runtime-native, text, skill, and plugin commands with surface-aware naming and serialized argument metadata.

features.name: Microsoft Teams Message Actions
features.description: Added message actions for pin, unpin, read, react, and listing reactions in Microsoft Teams.

features.name: Per-Provider Private Network Access
features.description: Added per-provider `models.providers.*.request.allowPrivateNetwork` for trusted self-hosted OpenAI-compatible endpoints, keeping the opt-in scoped to model request surfaces, and refreshing cached WebSocket managers when request transport overrides change.

features.name: Seedance 2.0 Model Refs
features.description: Adds Seedance 2.0 model refs to the bundled fal provider and submits the provider-specific duration, resolution, audio, and seed metadata fields needed for live Seedance 2.0 runs.

features.name: Multipass QA Runner Lane
features.description: Adds a `--runner multipass` lane for `openclaw qa suite` so repo-backed QA scenarios can run inside a disposable Linux VM and write back the usual report, summary, and VM logs.

features.name: ChatGPT Import Ingestion
features.description: Adds ChatGPT import ingestion plus new `Imported Insights` and `Memory Palace` diary subtabs so Dreaming can inspect imported source chats, compiled wiki pages, and full source pages directly from the UI.

features.name: Local Exec Policy Command
features.description: Adds a local `openclaw exec-policy` command with `show`, `preset`, and `set` subcommands for synchronizing requested `tools.exec.*` config with the local exec approvals file, plus follow-up hardening for node-host rejection, rollback safety, and sync conflict detection.

features.name: QA Multipass Runner
features.description: Adds a `--runner multipass` lane for `openclaw qa suite` so repo-backed QA scenarios can run inside a disposable Linux VM and write back the usual report, summary, and VM logs.

features.name: Plugin Manifest Activation and Setup Descriptors
features.description: Allow plugin manifests to declare activation and setup descriptors so plugin setup flows can describe required auth, pairing, and configuration steps without hardcoded core special cases.

features.name: URL-only Generated Asset Delivery
features.description: Add URL-only generated asset delivery for video generation tools.

features.name: Typed Provider Options
features.description: Add typed `providerOptions` for video generation tools.

features.name: Reference Audio Inputs
features.description: Add reference audio inputs for video generation tools.

features.name: Per-Asset Role Hints
features.description: Add per-asset role hints for video generation tools.

features.name: Adaptive Aspect Ratio Support
features.description: Add `adaptive` aspect-ratio support for video generation tools.

features.name: Higher Image Input Cap
features.description: Add a higher image-input cap for video generation tools.

features.name: Imported Insights and Memory Palace Diary Subtabs
features.description: Add `Imported Insights` and `Memory Palace` diary subtabs so Dreaming can inspect imported source chats, compiled wiki pages, and full source pages directly from the UI.

features.name: [embed ...] rich output tag
features.description: Adds the `[embed ...]` rich output tag for embedding external URLs in webchat, gated behind configuration.

features.name: GPT-5.4 vs Opus 4.6 Agentic Parity Report Gate
features.description: Adds the GPT-5.4 vs Opus 4.6 agentic parity report gate with shared scenario coverage checks, stricter evidence heuristics, and skipped-scenario accounting for maintainer review.

commands.name: openclaw onboard
commands.description: Guided setup wizard. Configures auth, gateway settings, and channels.

commands.name: openclaw dashboard
commands.description: Open Control UI in browser at http://127.0.0.1:18789/.

commands.name: openclaw tui
commands.description: Launch terminal UI for gateway monitoring.

commands.name: openclaw gateway status
commands.description: Add `openclaw gateway status --require-rpc` and clearer Linux non-interactive daemon-install failure reporting so automation can fail hard on probe misses instead of treating a printed RPC error as green.

commands.name: /approve
commands.description: Handles both exec and plugin approvals with automatic fallback.

commands.name: openclaw config schema
commands.description: Prints the generated JSON schema for openclaw.json.

commands.name: /tasks
commands.description: Chat-native background task board for the current session.

commands.name: /context detail
commands.description: Compares the tracked prompt estimate with cached context usage and surfaces untracked provider/runtime overhead when present.

commands.name: openclaw infer
commands.description: Hub for provider-backed inference workflows across model, media, web, and embedding tasks.

commands.name: openclaw exec-policy
commands.description: A local command with `show`, `preset`, and `set` subcommands for synchronizing requested `tools.exec.*` config with the local exec approvals file, plus follow-up hardening for node-host rejection, rollback safety, and sync conflict detection.

commands.name: openclaw qa matrix
commands.description: Starts a live QA lane backed by a disposable Matrix homeserver.

commands.name: openclaw qa telegram
commands.description: Starts a live QA lane for private-group bot-to-bot checks.

current.description: Force-kill listener on selected port then start.
Install Gateway as system daemon service during onboarding.
Run `openclaw` commands inside a running Docker or Podman OpenClaw container.
Replaces `gateway run --claude-cli-logs` with generic `--cli-backend-logs` while keeping the old flag as a compatibility alias.
Overrides built-in dangerous-code `critical` findings and install-time scan failures.
Per-job tool allowlists for cron jobs.
A flag for `openclaw qa suite` to run repo-backed QA scenarios inside a disposable Linux VM.
A flag for `openclaw` to route gateway-mode outbound WhatsApp sends through the channel `sendMedia` path and preserve media access context.

env_vars.name: OPENCLAW_TZ
env_vars.purpose: Pin gateway and CLI containers to a chosen IANA timezone instead of inheriting the daemon default.

env_vars.name: OPENCLAW_CONTAINER
env_vars.purpose: Run `openclaw` commands inside a running Docker or Podman OpenClaw container.

env_vars.name: ARCEEAI_API_KEY
env_vars.purpose: API key for Arcee AI provider.

models.note: OpenClaw is model-agnostic. Configure any provider/model in gateway config.
models.default_model_config: Set in gateway config: model field under provider section

breaking_changes.title: Escape invisible Unicode format characters in approval prompts
breaking_changes.description: Invisible Unicode format characters in approval prompts are now escaped so zero-width command text renders as visible `\u{...}` escapes instead of spoofing the reviewed command.
breaking_changes.migration: No migration needed.

breaking_changes.title: Normalize compatibility Unicode and strip invisible formatting code points before obfuscation checks
breaking_changes.description: Compatibility Unicode is now normalized and invisible formatting code points are stripped before obfuscation checks, so zero-width and fullwidth command tricks no longer suppress heuristic detection.
breaking_changes.migration: No migration needed.

breaking_changes.title: Preserve POSIX case sensitivity and keep `?` within a single path segment in exec allowlist
breaking_changes.description: POSIX case sensitivity is now preserved and `?` is kept within a single path segment, so exact-looking allowlist patterns no longer overmatch executables across case or directory boundaries.
breaking_changes.migration: Update allowlist patterns to be case-sensitive and ensure `?` only matches within a single path segment.

breaking_changes.title: Require sender ownership for `/config` and `/debug` commands
breaking_changes.description: Authorized non-owner senders can no longer reach owner-only config and runtime debug surfaces via `/config` and `/debug` commands. Only the owner can use these commands.
breaking_changes.migration: Only the owner can use `/config` and `/debug` commands.

breaking_changes.title: Clear unbound client-declared scopes on shared-token WebSocket connects
breaking_changes.description: Device-less shared-token operators cannot self-declare elevated scopes on shared-token WebSocket connects.
breaking_changes.migration: No migration needed.

breaking_changes.title: Block persistent browser profile create/delete routes from write-scoped `browser.request`
breaking_changes.description: Callers can no longer persist admin-only browser profile changes through the browser control surface using write-scoped `browser.request`.
breaking_changes.migration: No migration needed.

breaking_changes.title: Reject public spawned-run lineage fields and keep workspace inheritance on the internal spawned-session path
breaking_changes.description: External `agent` callers can no longer override the gateway workspace boundary.
breaking_changes.migration: No migration needed.

breaking_changes.title: Enforce sandbox session-tree visibility and shared agent-to-agent access guards before reading or mutating target session state
breaking_changes.description: Sandboxed subagents can no longer inspect parent session metadata or write parent model overrides via `session_status`.
breaking_changes.migration: No migration needed.

breaking_changes.title: Cap issued and verified device-token scopes to each paired device's approved scope baseline
breaking_changes.description: Stale or overbroad tokens cannot exceed approved access.
breaking_changes.migration: No migration needed.

breaking_changes.title: Shorten unauthenticated handshake retention and reject oversized pre-auth frames before application-layer parsing
breaking_changes.description: Reduces pre-pairing exposure on unsupported public deployments.
breaking_changes.migration: No migration needed.

breaking_changes.title: Restore the shared media-store size cap for persisted browser proxy files
breaking_changes.description: Oversized payloads are rejected instead of overriding the intended 5 MB limit.
breaking_changes.migration: No migration needed.

breaking_changes.title: Block inherited `GIT_EXEC_PATH` from sanitized host exec environments
breaking_changes.description: Git helper resolution cannot be steered by host environment state.
breaking_changes.migration: No migration needed.

breaking_changes.title: Require `encryptKey` alongside `verificationToken` in Feishu webhook mode
breaking_changes.description: Unsigned forged events are rejected instead of being processed with token-only configuration.
breaking_changes.migration: Configure `encryptKey` alongside `verificationToken` in Feishu webhook mode.

breaking_changes.title: Preserve looked-up group chat typing and fail closed on ambiguous reaction context
breaking_changes.description: Group authorization and mention gating cannot be bypassed through synthetic `p2p` reactions.
breaking_changes.migration: No migration needed.

breaking_changes.title: Require signatures for empty-event POST probes too
breaking_changes.description: Unsigned requests no longer confirm webhook reachability with a `200` response.
breaking_changes.migration: No migration needed.

breaking_changes.title: Rate limit invalid secret guesses before auth
breaking_changes.description: Weak webhook secrets cannot be brute-forced through unauthenticated churned requests without pre-auth `429` responses.
breaking_changes.migration: No migration needed.

breaking_changes.title: Require stable group IDs for allowlist auth by default and gate mutable group-name matching behind `channels.zalouser.dangerouslyAllowNameMatching`
breaking_changes.description: Stable group IDs are now required for allowlist auth by default. Mutable group-name matching is gated behind `channels.zalouser.dangerouslyAllowNameMatching`.
breaking_changes.migration: Use stable group IDs for allowlist auth or enable `channels.zalouser.dangerouslyAllowNameMatching` if mutable group-name matching is required.

breaking_changes.title: Require stable channel and team IDs for allowlist routing by default
breaking_changes.description: Mutable name matching is only available via each channel's `dangerouslyAllowNameMatching` break-glass flag.
breaking_changes.migration: Use stable channel and team IDs for allowlist routing or enable `dangerouslyAllowNameMatching` if mutable name matching is required.

breaking_changes.title: Fail closed for ambiguous inline loader and shell-payload script execution
breaking_changes.description: Fail closed for ambiguous inline loader and shell-payload script execution, bind the real script after POSIX shell value-taking flags, and unwrap `pnpm`/`npm exec`/`npx` script runners before approval binding.
breaking_changes.migration: No migration needed.

breaking_changes.title: Providers/Qwen: remove the deprecated `qwen-portal-auth` OAuth integration
breaking_changes.description: The `qwen-portal-auth` OAuth integration for `portal.qwen.ai` has been removed.
breaking_changes.migration: Migrate to Model Studio with `openclaw onboard --auth-choice modelstudio-api-key`.

breaking_changes.title: Config/Doctor: drop automatic config migrations older than two months
breaking_changes.description: Automatic config migrations older than two months have been dropped. Very old legacy keys now fail validation instead of being rewritten on load or by `openclaw doctor`.
affected: Users with very old OpenClaw configurations
breaking_changes.migration: Manually update your configuration to the latest schema.

breaking_changes.description: The deprecated `qwen-portal-auth` OAuth integration for `portal.qwen.ai` has been removed.
breaking_changes.migration: Migrate to Model Studio with `openclaw onboard --auth-choice modelstudio-api-key`.

affected: Users with very old OpenClaw configurations

affected: Users with very old openclaw.json configurations

affected: Users with very old openclaw.json config files

affected: Users with very old OpenClaw configurations

affected: Users with very old openclaw configurations
breaking_changes.migration: Manually update your configuration to the latest schema.

affected: Users with very old openclaw.json configurations

breaking_changes.description: The deprecated `qwen-portal-auth` OAuth integration for `portal.qwen.ai` has been removed.
breaking_changes.migration: Migrate to Model Studio with `openclaw onboard --auth-choice modelstudio-api-key`.

breaking_changes.title: Dropped Automatic Config Migrations Older Than Two Months
breaking_changes.description: Automatic config migrations older than two months have been dropped. Very old legacy keys now fail validation instead of being rewritten on load or by `openclaw doctor`.
breaking_changes.migration: Manually update your configuration to the latest schema.

breaking_changes.title: Old Config Migrations Dropped
breaking_changes.description: Automatic config migrations older than two months have been dropped. Very old legacy keys now fail validation instead of being rewritten on load or by `openclaw doctor`.
breaking_changes.migration: Manually update your configuration to the latest schema.

breaking_changes.title: Nodes/exec: remove the duplicated `nodes.run` shell wrapper
breaking_changes.description: The duplicated `nodes.run` shell wrapper has been removed from the CLI and agent `nodes` tool so node shell execution always goes through `exec host=node`, keeping node-specific capabilities on `nodes invoke` and the dedicated media/location/notify actions.
breaking_changes.migration: Use `exec host=node` for node shell execution.

breaking_changes.title: Plugin SDK: deprecate the legacy provider compat subpaths
breaking_changes.description: The legacy provider compat subpaths plus the older bundled provider setup and channel-runtime compatibility shims are deprecated. Migration warnings will be emitted.
breaking_changes.migration: Use the current documented `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` entrypoints plus local `api.ts` / `runtime-api.ts` barrels.

breaking_changes.title: Skills/install and Plugins/install: built-in dangerous-code `critical` findings and install-time scan failures now fail closed by default
breaking_changes.description: Built-in dangerous-code `critical` findings and install-time scan failures now fail closed by default, so plugin installs and gateway-backed skill dependency installs that previously succeeded may now require an explicit dangerous override.
breaking_changes.migration: Use `--dangerously-force-unsafe-install` to proceed with potentially unsafe installs.

breaking_changes.title: Gateway/auth: `trusted-proxy` now rejects mixed shared-token configs
breaking_changes.description: `trusted-proxy` now rejects mixed shared-token configs, and local-direct fallback requires the configured token instead of implicitly authenticating same-host callers.
breaking_changes.migration: Ensure `trusted-proxy` configurations use consistent shared tokens.

breaking_changes.title: Gateway/node commands: node commands now stay disabled until node pairing is approved
breaking_changes.description: Node commands now stay disabled until node pairing is approved, so device pairing alone is no longer enough to expose declared node commands.
breaking_changes.migration: Approve node pairing to enable node commands.

breaking_changes.title: Gateway/node events: node-originated runs now stay on a reduced trusted surface
breaking_changes.description: Node-originated runs now stay on a reduced trusted surface, so notification-driven or node-triggered flows that previously relied on broader host/session tool access may need adjustment.
breaking_changes.migration: Adjust notification-driven or node-triggered flows to use the reduced trusted surface.

breaking_changes.title: Nodes/exec: remove the duplicated `nodes.run` shell wrapper
breaking_changes.description: Remove the duplicated `nodes.run` shell wrapper from the CLI and agent `nodes` tool so node shell execution always goes through `exec host=node`, keeping node-specific capabilities on `nodes invoke` and the dedicated media/location/notify actions.
breaking_changes.migration: Use `exec host=node` for node shell execution.

breaking_changes.title: Plugin SDK: deprecate the legacy provider compat subpaths
breaking_changes.description: Deprecate the legacy provider compat subpaths plus the older bundled provider setup and channel-runtime compatibility shims, emit migration warnings, and keep the current documented `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` entrypoints plus local `api.ts` / `runtime-api.ts` barrels as the forward path ahead of a future major-release removal.
breaking_changes.migration: Use `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` entrypoints plus local `api.ts` / `runtime-api.ts` barrels.

breaking_changes.title: Skills/install and Plugins/install: built-in dangerous-code `critical` findings now fail closed by default
breaking_changes.description: Built-in dangerous-code `critical` findings and install-time scan failures now fail closed by default, so plugin installs and gateway-backed skill dependency installs that previously succeeded may now require an explicit dangerous override such as `--dangerously-force-unsafe-install` to proceed.
breaking_changes.migration: Use `--dangerously-force-unsafe-install` to proceed with installs that fail due to dangerous-code findings.

breaking_changes.title: Gateway/auth: `trusted-proxy` now rejects mixed shared-token configs
breaking_changes.description: `trusted-proxy` now rejects mixed shared-token configs, and local-direct fallback requires the configured token instead of implicitly authenticating same-host callers.
breaking_changes.migration: Ensure `trusted-proxy` configurations do not mix shared tokens and that local-direct fallbacks use the configured token.

breaking_changes.title: Gateway/node commands: node commands now stay disabled until node pairing is approved
breaking_changes.description: Node commands now stay disabled until node pairing is approved, so device pairing alone is no longer enough to expose declared node commands.
breaking_changes.migration: Approve node pairing to enable node commands.

breaking_changes.title: Gateway/node events: node-originated runs now stay on a reduced trusted surface
breaking_changes.description: Node-originated runs now stay on a reduced trusted surface, so notification-driven or node-triggered flows that previously relied on broader host/session tool access may need adjustment.
breaking_changes.migration: Adjust notification-driven or node-triggered flows that rely on broader host/session tool access.

breaking_changes.title: Skills/install and Plugins/install: built-in dangerous-code `critical` findings now fail closed by default
breaking_changes.description: Built-in dangerous-code `critical` findings and install-time scan failures now fail closed by default, so plugin installs and gateway-backed skill dependency installs that previously succeeded may now require an explicit dangerous override such as `--dangerously-force-unsafe-install` to proceed.
breaking_changes.migration: Use `--dangerously-force-unsafe-install` to proceed with installs that trigger dangerous-code findings.

breaking_changes.title: Gateway/node commands: node commands now stay disabled until node pairing is approved
breaking_changes.description: Node commands now stay disabled until node pairing is approved, so device pairing alone is no longer enough to expose declared node commands.
breaking_changes.migration: Approve node pairing to enable node commands.

breaking_changes.title: Nodes/exec: remove the duplicated `nodes.run` shell wrapper
breaking_changes.description: The duplicated `nodes.run` shell wrapper has been removed from the CLI and agent `nodes` tool so node shell execution always goes through `exec host=node`, keeping node-specific capabilities on `nodes invoke` and the dedicated media/location/notify actions.
breaking_changes.migration: Node shell execution should now go through `exec host=node`. Node-specific capabilities are available on `nodes invoke` and the dedicated media/location/notify actions.

breaking_changes.title: Plugin SDK: deprecate the legacy provider compat subpaths
breaking_changes.description: The legacy provider compat subpaths plus the older bundled provider setup and channel-runtime compatibility shims have been deprecated. Migration warnings will be emitted. The current documented `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` entrypoints plus local `api.ts` / `runtime-api.ts` barrels are the forward path ahead of a future major-release removal.
breaking_changes.migration: Migrate to the current documented `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` entrypoints plus local `api.ts` / `runtime-api.ts` barrels.

breaking_changes.title: Skills/install and Plugins/install: built-in dangerous-code `critical` findings and install-time scan failures now fail closed by default
breaking_changes.description: Built-in dangerous-code `critical` findings and install-time scan failures now fail closed by default, so plugin installs and gateway-backed skill dependency installs that previously succeeded may now require an explicit dangerous override such as `--dangerously-force-unsafe-install` to proceed.
breaking_changes.migration: If plugin installs and gateway-backed skill dependency installs fail, use the `--dangerously-force-unsafe-install` flag to proceed.

breaking_changes.title: Gateway/node commands: node commands now stay disabled until node pairing is approved
breaking_changes.description: Node commands now stay disabled until node pairing is approved, so device pairing alone is no longer enough to expose declared node commands.
breaking_changes.migration: Approve node pairing to enable node commands.

breaking_changes.title: Gateway/node events: node-originated runs now stay on a reduced trusted surface
breaking_changes.description: Node-originated runs now stay on a reduced trusted surface, so notification-driven or node-triggered flows that previously relied on broader host/session tool access may need adjustment.
breaking_changes.migration: Adjust notification-driven or node-triggered flows that relied on broader host/session tool access.

breaking_changes.title: Telegram group mentions configuration migration
breaking_changes.description: The `channels.telegram.groupMentionsOnly` configuration option has been removed. Legacy configurations using this option will be automatically migrated to `channels.telegram.groups["*"].requireMention`.
breaking_changes.migration: The `channels.telegram.groupMentionsOnly` setting has been migrated to `channels.telegram.groups["*"].requireMention`. No manual intervention is required, but you should update your configuration to use the new setting.

breaking_changes.title: Plugins/xAI: Config Migration
breaking_changes.description: Moves `x_search` settings from the legacy core `tools.web.x_search.*` path to the plugin-owned `plugins.entries.xai.config.xSearch.*` path, standardizes `x_search` auth on `plugins.entries.xai.config.webSearch.apiKey` / `XAI_API_KEY`.
breaking_changes.migration: Migrate legacy config with `openclaw doctor --fix`.

breaking_changes.title: Plugins/web fetch: Config Migration
breaking_changes.description: Moves Firecrawl `web_fetch` config from the legacy core `tools.web.fetch.firecrawl.*` path to the plugin-owned `plugins.entries.firecrawl.config.webFetch.*` path, routes `web_fetch` fallback through the new fetch-provider boundary instead of a Firecrawl-only core branch.
breaking_changes.migration: Migrate legacy config with `openclaw doctor --fix`.

breaking_changes.title: Plugins/xAI: move `x_search` settings
breaking_changes.description: The `x_search` settings have been moved from the legacy core `tools.web.x_search.*` path to the plugin-owned `plugins.entries.xai.config.xSearch.*` path. `x_search` auth is now standardized on `plugins.entries.xai.config.webSearch.apiKey` / `XAI_API_KEY`.
breaking_changes.migration: Migrate legacy config with `openclaw doctor --fix`.

breaking_changes.title: Plugins/web fetch: move Firecrawl `web_fetch` config
breaking_changes.description: The Firecrawl `web_fetch` config has been moved from the legacy core `tools.web.fetch.firecrawl.*` path to the plugin-owned `plugins.entries.firecrawl.config.webFetch.*` path. `web_fetch` fallback is now routed through the new fetch-provider boundary instead of a Firecrawl-only core branch.
breaking_changes.migration: Migrate legacy config with `openclaw doctor --fix`.

breaking_changes.title: Plugins/xAI: Migrate x_search config to plugin-owned path
breaking_changes.description: Moves `x_search` settings from the legacy core `tools.web.x_search.*` path to the plugin-owned `plugins.entries.xai.config.xSearch.*` path, standardizes `x_search` auth on `plugins.entries.xai.config.webSearch.apiKey` / `XAI_API_KEY`.
breaking_changes.migration: Migrate legacy config with `openclaw doctor --fix`.

breaking_changes.title: Plugins/web fetch: Migrate Firecrawl web_fetch config to plugin-owned path
breaking_changes.description: Moves Firecrawl `web_fetch` config from the legacy core `tools.web.fetch.firecrawl.*` path to the plugin-owned `plugins.entries.firecrawl.config.webFetch.*` path, routes `web_fetch` fallback through the new fetch-provider boundary instead of a Firecrawl-only core branch.
breaking_changes.migration: Migrate legacy config with `openclaw doctor --fix`.

breaking_changes.title: Config: Remove Legacy Public Config Aliases
breaking_changes.description: Removes legacy public config aliases such as `talk.voiceId` / `talk.apiKey`, `agents.*.sandbox.perSession`, `browser.ssrfPolicy.allowPrivateNetwork`, `hooks.internal.handlers`, and channel/group/room `allow` toggles in favor of the canonical public paths and `enabled`.
breaking_changes.migration: Use the canonical public paths and `enabled` instead. `openclaw doctor --fix` provides migration support for existing configs.

breaking_changes.title: Remove legacy public config aliases
breaking_changes.description: Removes legacy public config aliases such as `talk.voiceId` / `talk.apiKey`, `agents.*.sandbox.perSession`, `browser.ssrfPolicy.allowPrivateNetwork`, `hooks.internal.handlers`, and channel/group/room `allow` toggles.
breaking_changes.migration: Use the canonical public paths and `enabled`. `openclaw doctor --fix` provides migration support for existing configs.

breaking_changes.title: Remove legacy public config aliases
breaking_changes.description: Removes legacy public config aliases such as `talk.voiceId` / `talk.apiKey`, `agents.*.sandbox.perSession`, `browser.ssrfPolicy.allowPrivateNetwork`, `hooks.internal.handlers`, and channel/group/room `allow` toggles.
breaking_changes.migration: Use the canonical public paths and `enabled`. `openclaw doctor --fix` provides migration support for existing configs.

breaking_changes.title: Gateway Tool/Exec Config Restrictions
breaking_changes.description: Model-facing `gateway config.apply` and `config.patch` writes are now blocked from changing exec approval paths such as `safeBins`, `safeBinProfiles`, `safeBinTrustedDirs`, and `strictInlineEval`. This prevents malicious models from modifying security-sensitive configurations.
breaking_changes.migration: Ensure that exec approval paths are managed through secure channels and not directly exposed to model-driven configuration updates.

breaking_changes.title: Host Exec/Env Sanitization
breaking_changes.description: Dangerous Java, Rust, Cargo, Git, Kubernetes, cloud credential, config-path, and Helm env overrides are now blocked. This prevents host-run tools from being redirected to attacker-chosen code, config, credentials, or repository state.
breaking_changes.migration: Review and adjust any existing configurations that rely on these environment variables. Ensure that tools are not dependent on potentially malicious environment overrides.

breaking_changes.title: Network Fetch Guard: Redirect Bodies Dropped
breaking_changes.description: Request bodies and body-describing headers are now dropped on cross-origin `307` and `308` redirects by default. This prevents attacker-controlled redirect hops from receiving secret-bearing POST payloads from SSRF-guarded fetch flows unless a caller explicitly opts in.
breaking_changes.migration: If your application relies on forwarding request bodies across redirects, you will need to explicitly opt-in to this behavior. Review your fetch configurations and ensure that you understand the security implications of forwarding request bodies across redirects.

breaking_changes.title: Browser Node Invoke: Profile Mutations Blocked
breaking_changes.description: Persistent browser profile create, reset, and delete mutations through `browser.proxy` are now blocked on both gateway-forwarded `node.invoke` and the node-host proxy path, even when no profile allowlist is configured.
breaking_changes.migration: If your application relies on creating, resetting, or deleting browser profiles through `browser.proxy`, you will need to adjust your approach. Consider using alternative methods for managing browser profiles that do not involve persistent mutations.

breaking_changes.title: Gateway Auth: WebSocket Invalidation
breaking_changes.description: Existing shared-token and password WebSocket sessions are now invalidated when the configured secret rotates. This ensures that stale authenticated sockets cannot stay attached after token or password changes.
breaking_changes.migration: No specific migration steps are required. This change improves security by ensuring that WebSocket sessions are properly invalidated after authentication secrets are rotated.

breaking_changes.title: Agents/gateway-tool: Reject config.patch and config.apply calls that enable security-sensitive flags
breaking_changes.description: The model-facing gateway tool now rejects `config.patch` and `config.apply` calls that would newly enable any flag enumerated by `openclaw security audit` (e.g., `dangerouslyDisableDeviceAuth`, `allowInsecureAuth`, `dangerouslyAllowHostHeaderOriginFallback`, `hooks.gmail.allowUnsafeExternalContent`, `tools.exec.applyPatch.workspaceOnly: false`). This prevents models from escalating privileges through the gateway tool.
affected: Users relying on the model-facing gateway tool to enable security-sensitive flags.
breaking_changes.migration: Already-enabled flags pass through unchanged, so non-dangerous edits in the same patch still apply. Direct authenticated operator RPC behavior is unchanged. Review your gateway tool configurations and ensure that no security-sensitive flags are being enabled through the model-facing gateway tool.

breaking_changes.title: Browser/SSRF: Preserve explicit strict browser navigation mode for legacy configs
breaking_changes.description: Explicit strict browser navigation mode for legacy `browser.ssrfPolicy.allowPrivateNetwork: false` configs is now preserved by normalizing the legacy alias to the canonical strict marker instead of silently widening those installs to the default non-strict hostname-navigation path. This ensures that existing strict SSRF policies are not unintentionally relaxed.
breaking_changes.migration: No direct migration steps are required, but verify that your browser SSRF policy remains as intended after the upgrade.

breaking_changes.title: Browser SSRF Policy Normalization
breaking_changes.description: Explicit strict browser navigation mode for legacy `browser.ssrfPolicy.allowPrivateNetwork: false` configs is now normalized to the canonical strict marker instead of silently widening those installs to the default non-strict hostname-navigation path. This ensures that existing configurations that explicitly disabled private network access for browser navigation continue to do so.
breaking_changes.migration: If you relied on the previous behavior where `browser.ssrfPolicy.allowPrivateNetwork: false` implicitly allowed hostname navigation, you may need to explicitly configure the desired SSRF policy.

breaking_changes.title: Feishu Allowlist Canonicalization
breaking_changes.description: Feishu allowlist entries are now canonicalized by explicit `user`/`chat` kind, repeated `feishu:`/`lark:` provider prefixes are stripped, and opaque Feishu IDs are no longer folded to lowercase. This prevents allowlist matching from crossing user/chat namespaces or widening to case-insensitive ID matches the operator did not intend.
breaking_changes.migration: Review your Feishu allowlist entries to ensure they are correctly specified with explicit `user`/`chat` kinds and that opaque IDs are case-sensitive. Remove any redundant `feishu:`/`lark:` prefixes.

breaking_changes.title: Gateway Tool Config Patch Security
breaking_changes.description: The model-facing gateway tool now rejects `config.patch` and `config.apply` calls that would newly enable flags enumerated by `openclaw security audit` (e.g., `dangerouslyDisableDeviceAuth`). Already-enabled flags pass through unchanged, and direct authenticated operator RPC behavior is unchanged.
affected: Users using the model-facing gateway tool to patch configurations.
breaking_changes.migration: Ensure that any necessary flags are enabled through direct authenticated operator RPC calls instead of the model-facing gateway tool.

breaking_changes.title: Browser SSRF Policy Normalization
breaking_changes.description: Explicit strict browser navigation mode for legacy `browser.ssrfPolicy.allowPrivateNetwork: false` configs is now normalized to the canonical strict marker instead of silently widening those installs to the default non-strict hostname-navigation path.
breaking_changes.migration: Verify that the browser SSRF policy is correctly configured after the update.

breaking_changes.title: Restrict `config.patch` and `config.apply` calls from model-facing gateway tool
breaking_changes.description: The model-facing gateway tool now rejects `config.patch` and `config.apply` calls that would newly enable flags enumerated by `openclaw security audit` (e.g., `dangerouslyDisableDeviceAuth`, `allowInsecureAuth`, `dangerouslyAllowHostHeaderOriginFallback`, `hooks.gmail.allowUnsafeExternalContent`, `tools.exec.applyPatch.workspaceOnly: false`). Already-enabled flags pass through unchanged, and direct authenticated operator RPC behavior is unchanged.
affected: Users relying on the model-facing gateway tool to enable potentially dangerous flags.
breaking_changes.migration: Review and adjust configurations to ensure no critical functionality relies on enabling restricted flags via the model-facing gateway tool. Consider alternative methods for enabling these flags if necessary, such as direct operator RPC calls.

support_matrix.agent_config_format: YAML frontmatter + SOUL.md + MEMORY.md in agent workspace

key_sections.agents: Named agent definitions with model, system prompt, channels
key_sections.providers: LLM provider API keys and settings
